title: The On-Chain Architecture
description: Follow the data through tries, signatures, commitments, and beacons — how the protocol actually works.

stops.title: Three Tries, Three Concerns
stops.narrative:
The on-chain architecture is built around three Merkle Patricia Tries, each owned by a different party.

The [identity trie](node:identity-trie) holds attested actor public keys (owned by the [identity provider](node:identity-provider)). The [regulation trie](node:regulation-trie) holds qualifications and standing (owned by the [regulator](node:regulator)). The [process trie](node:process-trie) holds items and state (owned by the [operator](node:operator)).

The [smart contract](node:smart-contract) ties them together, reading identity and regulation tries as reference inputs while governing the process trie.

stops.title: MPT-per-Operator: The Storage Pattern
stops.narrative:
[MPT-per-operator](node:mpt-per-operator) is what makes scale economically viable. One UTxO per operator, regardless of item count. A manufacturer updating 10,000 batteries pays for one root hash update, not 10,000 transactions.

This satisfies [sequential access](node:constraint-sequential-access) by design — one writer per trie, no contention — and [data cadence](node:constraint-data-cadence) because the on-chain rate decouples from item volume.

stops.title: The Signing Function
stops.narrative:
The atomic unit is the [signing function](node:signing-function) — an opaque capability that produces signatures without exposing the private key. It can live on hardware (SE050 secure element for [physical mode](node:physical-mode)) or software (server endpoint for [process mode](node:process-mode)).

The [operator](node:operator) mints signing functions and distributes them. The [user](node:user) uses them. All substrates present the same interface to the chain: a public key and valid signatures.

stops.title: The Double Signature
stops.narrative:
Every state transition requires two signatures via the [double signature](node:double-signature) pattern.

The process signature (from the [signing function](node:signing-function)) proves authentication: this specific process produced this data at this time. The actor signature proves authorization: this party requests this transition.

The [smart contract](node:smart-contract) verifies both. Without the process signature, anyone could claim anything. Without the actor signature, anyone could trigger the signing function.

stops.title: Commitment-then-Submit
stops.narrative:
[Commitment-then-submit](node:commitment-protocol) is the mechanism that proves timeliness. The operator creates a commitment in a trie leaf with a slot window (validFrom, validUntil). The action clears the commitment. Single-use, slot-bounded, no off-chain timestamp trust.

This pattern is critical for GDPR's [72-hour breach notification](node:gdpr-breach-notification) and [1-month rights response](node:gdpr-rights-requests). It turns self-reported deadlines into verifiable on-chain facts via [temporal integrity](node:temporal-integrity).

stops.title: The Beacon Protocol
stops.narrative:
The [beacon](node:beacon) bridges the regulator's assessment to the user. The [operator](node:operator) mints it — the minting policy forces inclusion of the operator's current standing from the [regulation trie](node:regulation-trie).

The [user](node:user) verifies it is current and authentic using the regulator's published beacon policy identifier and trust anchor, then signs their data over it. This is [informed consent by construction](node:informed-consent) — the user cannot claim ignorance of disclosed information. The beacon expires, preventing stale reputation.

stops.title: The Baton Pattern
stops.narrative:
The [baton pattern](node:baton-pattern) handles authorization transfer. Actor A's final submission includes Actor B's public key, atomically transferring the right to act. No gap, no registration, no login.

In the [Battery Regulation](node:battery-regulation), the baton passes from manufacturer to transporter to consumer. In [GDPR](node:gdpr), the controller creates a commitment when a [rights request](node:gdpr-rights-requests) arrives and the baton designates who responds.

stops.title: Lifecycle State Machine
stops.narrative:
The [lifecycle state machine](node:lifecycle-state-machine) models status transitions as a field in the MPT leaf, validated on-chain with the invariant that no backward transitions occur unless explicitly allowed.

For [batteries](node:battery-regulation): Virgin to Active to Repurposed to Recycled. For [GDPR consent](node:gdpr-consent): None to Given to Withdrawn. Same pattern, different domain — the architecture does not change.
